stories.headline: NBI official testifies at Duterte impeachment trial as prosecution presents second witness
stories.summary: The House prosecution presented its second witness, National Bureau of Investigation regional director Jeremy Lotoc, who testified on the impeachment article covering alleged grave threats against President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., First Lady Liza Araneta-Marcos and former House Speaker Martin Romualdez. Lotoc said investigators had not found validated information identifying anyone allegedly hired to carry out the threats but maintained that the threat was serious and credible. The defense questioned his reliance on notes while testifying.
stories.context: The trial, which opened July 6, is the first impeachment of a sitting Philippine vice president and stems partly from remarks Duterte made at a November 2024 online press conference. A conviction requires a two-thirds Senate vote and would remove her from office. She has denied the accusations.
stories.watch_for: The prosecution is set to continue presenting witnesses in hearings through the week.

stories.headline: Death toll from Typhoon Inday and monsoon rises to 20 as over 650,000 affected
stories.summary: The National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council said the combined effects of Typhoon Inday and the enhanced southwest monsoon had left 20 people dead, though only one death — a 70-year-old man who drowned in Bukidnon — had been validated as of July 13. It said 16 people were missing and nine injured, and that about 146,000 families, or more than 654,000 people, had been affected nationwide, with more than 5,100 families in evacuation centers.
stories.context: Typhoon Inday left the Philippine Area of Responsibility earlier in the week but enhanced the seasonal southwest monsoon, or habagat, bringing days of heavy rain, flooding and landslides across the Visayas and Mindanao.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA said the habagat would keep bringing scattered rains and thunderstorms over Metro Manila, much of Luzon and the Western Visayas on July 13, with possible flash floods and landslides.

stories.headline: Marcos to make working visit to Singapore to court investors
stories.summary: Malacanang said President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. would travel to Singapore for a working visit from July 14 to 16 to strengthen bilateral ties and promote investment. He is scheduled to meet Prime Minister Lawrence Wong and take part in a Milken Institute roundtable with business executives, part of efforts to attract investors after the World Bank reclassified the Philippines as an upper-middle-income economy. First Lady Liza Araneta-Marcos is to address a related economic summit.
stories.context: Singapore is due to chair the Association of Southeast Asian Nations in 2027.
stories.watch_for: The visit runs July 14 to 16 and includes a planned meeting with Prime Minister Lawrence Wong.

stories.headline: Officials push for stronger maritime resilience after 10th anniversary of arbitral ruling
stories.summary: A day after the Philippines marked the 10th anniversary of the 2016 arbitral ruling that rejected China's expansive South China Sea claims, Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. said commemorations should translate into stronger resilience, deterrence, and infrastructure and cyber security, and urged local governments to move beyond symbolic resolutions. He said he was open to declaring July 12 a "Maritime Victory Day." Cebu officials called for concrete action to defend the country's maritime rights.
stories.context: The Permanent Court of Arbitration ruled in July 2016 that China's nine-dash-line claim had no basis under international law. China rejects the ruling. Manila calls waters within its exclusive economic zone the West Philippine Sea.

stories.headline: Philippine inflation eases to 6.4% in June but stays above target
stories.summary: The Philippine Statistics Authority reported that headline inflation slowed to 6.4% in June from 6.8% in May, which it attributed mainly to easing transport costs as global oil prices fell. The rate stayed above the government's 2 to 4 percent target, and average inflation for the first half of 2026 stood at 4.8%. The June figure was within the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas' forecast range of 6 to 7 percent.
stories.context: Philippine inflation rose through the first half of 2026 amid higher fuel costs linked to conflict in the Middle East, reaching a three-year high of 7.2% in April before easing in May and June.

stories.headline: Quezon City bridge closed after fire beneath it damages structure
stories.summary: A fourth-alarm fire that broke out on the night of July 11 destroyed dozens of homes belonging to informal settler families beneath Culiat Bridge II along Congressional Avenue in Quezon City. No injuries were reported among residents, who were moved to a nearby elementary school. Authorities closed the bridge to motorists after parts of the structure were found to have lifted from the intense heat, rerouting traffic while engineers assess whether it is safe to reopen.
stories.watch_for: The Quezon City government said it was awaiting recommendations from the Department of Public Works and Highways on how long the bridge would remain closed.

stories.headline: Mayon Volcano produces brief lava fountaining as Alert Level 3 continues
stories.summary: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said Mayon Volcano in Albay produced a brief episode of lava fountaining from its summit crater for about two minutes on the afternoon of July 13, and recorded a pyroclastic density current earlier that morning. Alert Level 3 remains in effect and entry into the six-kilometer permanent danger zone is prohibited. Phivolcs said elevated sulfur dioxide emissions had raised the possibility of a more explosive eruption.
stories.context: Phivolcs says Mayon has been in a state of sustained eruptive activity for more than 180 days, producing lava flows, rockfalls and pyroclastic density currents.

stories.headline: Marcos proclaims new economic zones in Cavite, Batangas and Negros Oriental
stories.summary: Newly released proclamations signed on the President's authority designate three new special economic zones, the Inquirer reported. They cover a Suntrust ecotown zone in Tanza, Cavite; a Light Industry and Science Park zone in Malvar, Batangas; and a Robinsons information-technology park in Dumaguete City, Negros Oriental. The zones, endorsed by the Philippine Economic Zone Authority, are intended to draw locators and investment.

stories.headline: Fire at Bangkok pub kills at least 27, injures dozens
stories.summary: At least 27 people were killed and more than 60 injured when a fire tore through the Na Ladprao pub in northern Bangkok around midnight on July 12, Thai officials said. Firefighters brought the blaze under control within about half an hour. Police said they were investigating possible negligence, including obstructed exits, while a preliminary line of inquiry pointed to a possible electrical fault. Many of the victims were found near the restrooms at the back of the venue.
stories.context: Officials described it as one of the deadliest fires in the Thai capital in years.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade fresh strikes as Strait of Hormuz standoff escalates
stories.summary: The US military carried out a second consecutive day of strikes on Iranian targets, while Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it had hit US installations in Gulf states including Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan and Oman, according to Al Jazeera. Iran says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz to shipping, a claim the United States disputes even as vessel traffic through the waterway fell sharply. One person was reported killed in Iran's Khuzestan province.
stories.context: A ceasefire reached last month briefly halted the conflict before fighting resumed in early July; US President Donald Trump declared the interim agreement over on July 12. The Strait of Hormuz carries a large share of the world's seaborne oil.

stories.headline: World Cup semifinals set with Spain-France and Argentina-England
stories.summary: The semifinal lineup for the 2026 World Cup is set, with Spain to face France and defending champions Argentina to meet England. Argentina reached the last four by beating Switzerland 3-1 after extra time, while England advanced past Norway. The four remaining teams are the top four sides in FIFA's world rankings.
stories.watch_for: Spain face France on Tuesday, July 14 in Dallas, and Argentina meet England on Wednesday, July 15 in Atlanta.
